topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printout lay crumpled on the kitchen counter, the stark lettering of the genetic markers mocking me. My hands trembled so violently I had to grip the edge of the granite countertop to steady them. Every line seemed to scream a betrayal, a sudden, unwelcome realization that the foundation of my entire life was built on a lie. I stared at the results, the names listed beside the matches, and a cold, hollow ache settled deep in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I pushed the paper away, the sound a sharp rip in the quiet morning, and walked to the window, watching the familiar street, suddenly feeling utterly alien in my own skin.

[story 2]

I kept re-reading the report, the words swimming before my eyes, blurring into meaningless shapes. A tightness began to constrict around my throat, making swallowing a physical effort. I paced the length of the living room three times, my footsteps echoing too loudly in the silence. When I finally sat down on the sofa, I buried my face in my hands, the fabric of the cushions rough against my cheek. A low, ragged sound escaped my lips—more of a whimper than a word—as a wave of profound disorientation washed over me, leaving me feeling unsteady, as if the very ground beneath my feet had shifted.

[story 3]

The phone call had ended, leaving a ringing silence in its wake that felt deafening. I stood by the hallway mirror, my reflection staring back—a stranger whose eyes held a sudden, raw vulnerability. I slowly brought a hand up to touch my temple, as if trying to physically hold myself together. The air in the room felt thick and heavy, suffocating. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscles protesting beneath my skin. I wanted to scream, to shatter something, but the sound caught somewhere behind my teeth, leaving me suspended in a state of overwhelming, paralyzing shock.
